label: Marcus & Aaliyah
note: Marcus is a 7-year-old boy entering care following neglect by his primary caregiver. He has a younger sister, Aaliyah, age 5, and the siblings are deeply bonded — separating them would cause significant additional trauma. Marcus has a diagnosed asthma condition requiring a nebulizer and occasional ER visits. He is currently in 2nd grade at Lincoln Elementary in the Madison Unified district and his teacher reports he is performing at grade level despite home instability. Marcus speaks only English. He has been referred for trauma-focused CBT and ideally would be placed within 20 minutes of a licensed therapist. No dietary restrictions noted.

label: Sofia
note: Sofia is a 9-year-old girl, Spanish-speaking with limited English. She was removed from a home with domestic violence present. Sofia shows signs of anxiety and selective mutism in unfamiliar environments. She follows a halal diet and this is important to her sense of identity and comfort. No siblings in the system. She is in 4th grade and would benefit from placement stability near her current school. A Spanish-speaking household is strongly preferred — she shuts down entirely when she cannot communicate.

label: Devon
note: Devon is a 14-year-old male with a history of three prior placements, all of which broke down within 6 months. He has been diagnosed with ADHD and ODD. Devon is verbal about not wanting to be in care and has run away twice. He does well with clear structure and responds to male role models. No medical needs beyond his ADHD medication. English only. No siblings. Currently in 9th grade, behind by one year. Prior placement notes flag that overcrowded homes trigger escalation.
